title: FG defining function and Laplacian model
summary: Defines Q = 2ρt² in chosen-scale normal coordinates and packages the Euler operator and ambient Laplacian together with the algebraic laws required downstream.

mathematicalContent.heading: Defining function in normal form
mathematicalContent.body: A chosen scale trivializes the ray coordinate and supplies t > 0. In straight normal form, the ambient defining function is the squared norm of the dilation field and has the concrete expression Q = 2ρt².

mathematicalContent.heading: Certified operator model
mathematicalContent.body: NormalLaplacianModel stores a formal dimension n, an Euler operator X, and an operator denoted by Δ̃. Its remaining fields are proofs of linearity and the required Q–X–Δ̃ identities. Thus downstream theorems are valid under this model interface; this file does not construct Δ̃ from g̃.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This map forgets the positivity proof and regards a positive real number as an ordinary real number.
For a chosen scale s, scaleT is the positive ray coordinate t obtained by trivializing the metric-bundle component of an ambient point U.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def scaleT (s : Conf.Scale) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The normal-form defining function assigns 2ρ(U)t(U)² to each formal ambient point.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def normalQ (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) : Operators.Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluating normalQ gives its defining normal-coordinate formula.
correspondence.leanType: theorem normalQ_apply (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : normalQ A U = 2 * Conf.rhoCoord U * (scaleT A.baseScale U) ^ 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is pointwise multiplication of an ambient function by the normal-form defining function Q_A.
correspondence.leanType: def normalQmul (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) (f : Operators.Function Conf) : Operators.Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is pointwise multiplication by the m-th power of Q_A.
correspondence.leanType: def normalQpowMul (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) (m : ℕ) (f : Operators.Function Conf) : Operators.Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A certified model supplies n, X, and Δ̃ together with proofs of scalar linearity, additivity, the X–Δ̃ commutator, the Q commutator, and the homogeneous Q^m identity. These laws are assumptions of the model.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}XQ_A&=2Q_A,\qquad \widetilde\Delta(cf)=c\widetilde\Delta f,\qquad \widetilde\Delta(f+g)=\widetilde\Delta f+\widetilde\Delta g,\\X\widetilde\Delta f&=\widetilde\Delta Xf-2\widetilde\Delta f,\qquad \widetilde\Delta(Q_Af)=Q_A\widetilde\Delta f+2\bigl(2Xf+(n+2)f\bigr),\\Xf=wf&\Longrightarrow\widetilde\Delta(Q_A^mf)=Q_A^m\widetilde\Delta f+2m(2w+n+2m)Q_A^{m-1}f.\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The function Q_A is exactly the normal-form expression 2ρt² at every ambient point.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightForm_Q_eq (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) : normalQ A = fun U => 2 * Conf.rhoCoord U * (scaleT A.baseScale U) ^ 2

references.locator: Chapter 3, defining function, Euler field, and ambient Laplacian in normal form
references.note: Source for Q = 2ρt² and the displayed operator identities.

limitations: Only Q is concretely defined from normal coordinates. X and the ambient Laplacian are supplied as abstract functions, and all commutator and Q-power laws are fields of NormalLaplacianModel rather than consequences derived from a connection or metric.
